pattern: Sort the paths lexicographically so every sub-folder immediately follows its parent, then scan once keeping the last accepted ancestor to filter out children.
hints: If you sort the folder list, every sub-folder of a given parent must appear directly after that parent in sorted order.
A path B is a sub-folder of A only when B starts with A followed by a slash; the trailing slash guards against a prefix like /a matching /ab.
Walk through the sorted list keeping track of the last path you accepted; skip the current path whenever it starts with that last-accepted path plus '/'.

edge_inputs.description: one path is nested inside another, one is independent
all paths under a single root
prefix match must require trailing slash — /a/b/ca is not under /a/b/c
multiple levels of nesting — only top-level survives
siblings that share a prefix should all survive

stress.description: wide tree: many short top-level paths each with several sub-folders
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, size)
    roots = ['/r{}'.format(i) for i in range(min(50, n // 4 + 1))]
    paths = set(roots[:])
    while len(paths) < n:
        base = rng.choice(roots)
        depth = rng.randint(1, 4)
        p = base
        for _ in range(depth):
            p += '/' + rng.choice(['a','b','c','d','e','f','g'])
        paths.add(p)
    return [list(paths)]
